reasoning:
The resolution criteria depend on whether the RRPONTSYAWARD rate increases compared to its June 22, 2025 value (currently flat at 4.25%). This rate is administratively determined by the FOMC and typically moves in lock-step with the target range for the Federal Funds Rate. An increase would require an FOMC rate hike or a rare upward technical adjustment. 
For June 29 and July 22, there are no scheduled FOMC meetings between June 22 and July 22 (the next meeting is July 29-30). An emergency hike or a surprise off-cycle technical adjustment is extremely unlikely (set at the 0.05 minimum allowed). 
For the horizon reaching September 20, there are two scheduled meetings (July, September). However, inflation remains relatively steady and market expectations derived from Fed funds futures (as per the CME FedWatch tool) overwhelmingly favor rate cuts or holds for 2025. Hikes are universally seen as a tail-risk event, meaning the probability of an increase by September remains very low, assigned at 0.08. 
By December 19, two more meetings will have occurred (October and December). Extending the timeframe increases uncertainty, incorporating slightly more risk that persistent or resurgent inflation (e.g., from tariffs) forces the Fed's hand into a rate hike by the end of 2025. I assign a slightly elevated but still highly unlikely probability of 0.12.
